authoritative_sources.title: Interagency Guidance on Third-Party Relationships: Risk Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, FDIC, and OCC
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary interagency banking guidance for third-party lifecycle management, documentation, monitoring, independent review, and escalation across bank-fintech relationships.

authoritative_sources.title: Conducting Due Diligence on Financial Technology Companies: A Guide for Community Banks
authoritative_sources.publisher: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, FDIC, and OCC
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Joint banking-agency guide that operationalizes what evidence, controls, and operating details banks should collect when evaluating fintech partners and service relationships.

authoritative_sources.title: Joint Statement on Banks’ Arrangements with Third Parties to Deliver Bank Deposit Products and Services
authoritative_sources.publisher: FDIC, Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, and OCC
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Current interagency statement directly focused on bank-third-party deposit-product arrangements and the risk-management practices expected in those embedded-finance delivery models.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Deposit and wallet program boundary setting
used_for: routing when third-party product delivery changes ownership

authoritative_sources.title: Electronic Fund Transfers FAQs
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CFPB Regulation E guidance that clarifies when non-bank P2P and wallet providers are financial institutions and how unauthorized transfers and error-resolution duties affect embedded-finance workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: FedACH Processing Schedule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Federal Reserve operations schedule for ACH transmission deadlines, target distributions, and settlement timing that directly constrains posting and reconciliation cutoffs.

authoritative_sources.title: Same Day ACH Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Federal Reserve FAQ for return, reversal, NOC, fee, and effective-entry-date behavior that materially affects ACH exception handling and reconciliation logic.

authoritative_sources.title: 12 CFR Part 370 Recordkeeping for Timely Deposit Insurance Determination
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary FDIC recordkeeping framework for complete and accurate deposit-account information, ownership-right-and-capacity data, and IT capability relevant to embedded-finance deposit or wallet structures.
used_for: refusal when ownership or account truth is incomplete

authoritative_sources.title: Part 370 Alternative Recordkeeping Entity Processing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FDIC processing guide for pass-through and alternative recordkeeping entity arrangements, including file-format, timing, and supporting-document requirements relevant to brokered, custodial, and subaccounted embedded-finance programs.

authoritative_sources.title: Requirements for Custodial Deposit Accounts with Transactional Features and Prompt Payment of Deposit Insurance to Depositors
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FDIC proposed rule describing emerging expectations for beneficial-owner records, daily reconciliations, direct access, and independent validation in transactional custodial arrangements; used to constrain ambiguity rather than to assert final obligations.
used_for: runtime caveats for evolving custodial-account requirements

authoritative_sources.title: PCI DSS
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized payment-card security standard defining baseline operational and technical requirements for entities that store, process, transmit, or can impact payment-card data.

workflow_stages.name: Program, rail, and ledger topology scoping
workflow_stages.description: Identify the sponsor bank, program manager, processor, payment rails, funding account structure, FBO or custodial model, ledger of record, and settlement windows before any financial handling is attempted.

workflow_stages.name: Financial trigger and posting-support validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate that transactions, fees, reserves, disputes, returns, or approvals support a requested charge, invoice, posting, or adjustment using authoritative bank, processor, ledger, and policy records.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Submission, settlement, and reconciliation review
workflow_stages.description: Compare processor settlement files, sponsor-bank or treasury statements, FBO or custodial subaccount balances, ACH schedules, and internal ledger outcomes to identify matched items, timing differences, and unresolved variances.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception handling and closeout
workflow_stages.description: Package delayed files, duplicate events, unsupported adjustments, return or reversal problems, chargebacks, reserve discrepancies, and unresolved variances into escalation artifacts with evidence and next-owner routing.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Authority-boundary escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate engineering changes, partner-bank approvals, legal or compliance interpretation, formal accounting treatment, tax treatment, or AML or fraud adjudication outside this lane.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Sponsor-bank product approvals, program agreements, and partner operating procedures
Processor settlement files, payout or fee reports, reserve schedules, and transaction exports
Sponsor-bank operating account, settlement account, FBO account, or custodial account statements
Subledger or wallet ledger extracts, GL interface files, suspense-account detail, and reconciliation workpapers
ACH return, reversal, NOC, and effective-entry-date evidence
Chargeback, dispute, complaint, and return case artifacts
Beneficial-owner, subaccount, or pass-through deposit files where applicable
Webhook delivery logs, file-transmission confirmations, and change-ticket references
Approval histories for fees, reserves, write-offs, and manual interventions
Tenant policy extracts for posting authority, reserve handling, and escalation routing
systems_of_record: Sponsor-bank core banking or deposit platform
ACH operator or FedACH reporting tools
Risk, fraud, AML, and compliance systems when exceptions cross boundaries
Document management, contract, and policy repositories

allowed: Classify requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing embedded-finance prerequisites.
Determine whether source records support a charge, invoice, posting, reserve movement, or adjustment request.
Prepare billing or posting summaries, reconciliation notes, blocker lists, and exception escalation packets with explicit traceability.
Separate timing differences from true mismatches and route unresolved variances to the proper owner.
escalate: Sponsor-bank, processor, rail, FBO or custodial structure, or ledger of record is unclear.
Bank statements, processor reports, subledger entries, and GL outcomes materially disagree.
A policy or public-source conflict changes how disputes, returns, or consumer-impact handling should proceed.
The request requires partner-bank approval, engineering reconfiguration, accounting treatment, treasury movement, or legal or compliance interpretation.
refuse: Requests to back-date, suppress, or invent financial treatment without source support and authority.
Requests for engineering changes, legal interpretation, formal accounting treatment, tax guidance, or partner-bank signoff.
Requests that use the embedded-finance label but omit the actual sponsor-bank, processor, rail, ledger, or account-structure context.

domain_failure_modes: Treating embedded-finance integration as generic systems work and omitting sponsor-bank, processor, settlement-account, or FBO-account dependencies.
Posting or adjusting financial outcomes from processor summaries alone without reconciling sponsor-bank statements, subledger truth, or approval records.
Using unresolved ACH return, reversal, cutoff, or effective-entry-date issues to justify unsupported posting corrections.
Blurring operational reconciliation support with formal accounting, tax, legal, compliance, or partner-bank approval authority.
Ignoring custodial, pass-through, or beneficial-owner recordkeeping requirements in wallet or deposit programs and overstating readiness.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Source records do not support the requested billing or adjustment
Reconciliation mismatch or denial requires exception handling
Stakeholder asks for an unsupported financial override

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Evolving custodial deposit account recordkeeping expectations
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The FDIC's 2024 proposal on custodial deposit accounts with transactional features is authoritative as a statement of possible future direction, but it is not final law. The pack therefore treats daily reconciliation, direct record access, and annual validation for these structures as strong runtime caveats and escalates when a tenant depends on future-state obligations.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Rail-specific exception handling varies by processor, network, and bank program design
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources define ACH deadlines, return and reversal handling, and general bank-fintech controls, but exact file layouts, card-network reporting, reserve-release timing, and dispute-routing obligations vary by sponsor bank and processor. The pack requires tenant retrieval of those operating docs before final posting or override handling.

notes: The source mix covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling rather than only one dimension.

No blocking ambiguity remains for validated packaging because unsettled custodial-account rules and processor-specific operating details are treated as runtime prerequisites and escalation triggers, not hidden assumptions.
